warmups.name: Lateral Lunge Weight Shifts
warmups.desc: Targets: Hip Adductors & Glutes. Biomechanics: Gently shift weight side-to-side to lubricate joints and warm adductors. - Stand with feet wide, toes forward. - Keep chest up and push hips back as you shift weight to one side. - Cue: "Imagine sliding side-to-side on a slippery floor."

warmups.name: Dynamic Leg Swings
warmups.desc: Targets: Hip Flexors & Extensors. Biomechanics: Lubricates the entire hip capsule. - Hold onto a support. Swing one leg forward and back like a pendulum. - Keep the swinging leg relaxed. - Cue: "Feel the stretch at the top of the forward and backward swing."

compound.name: Side Lunge
compound.desc: Targets: Gluteus Medius & Adductor Magnus. Biomechanics: Lateral force loads the gluteus medius for hip stabilization. - Step out to the side, keeping the trailing leg straight. - Push your hips back as if sitting in a chair behind you. - Cue: "Push the floor away with your bent leg to return to center." This engages the glute medius.

isolations.name: Seated Hip Abduction Machine
isolations.desc: Targets: Gluteus Medius & Minimus. Biomechanics: Direct resistance against hip abduction isolates the side glutes. - Sit tall, chest up. Drive the pads outward with the sides of your legs. - Control the weight on the way back in; don't let it slam. - Cue: "Squeeze from the outside of your hips, not by pushing with your feet."

isolations.name: Cable Adduction
isolations.desc: Targets: Hip Adductor Group. Biomechanics: Pulling the leg across the midline directly isolates the inner thigh muscles. - Stand sideways to the machine. Pull the cabled leg across the front of your standing leg. - Move slowly and deliberately. - Cue: "Imagine squeezing a ball between your thighs."

cooldowns.name: Butterfly Stretch
cooldowns.desc: Targets: Hip Adductor Group. Biomechanics: Lengthens adductors by moving insertions away from the origin. - Sit on the floor, bring soles of your feet together. - Gently press your knees toward the floor. - Cue: "Let gravity do the work. Breathe and relax into the stretch."

cooldowns.name: Kneeling Quad Stretch
cooldowns.desc: Targets: Rectus Femoris & Hip Flexors. Biomechanics: Hip extension while the knee is flexed puts a maximal stretch on the quad. - Kneel on one knee. Lean forward slightly. - Gently grab the ankle of the back leg and pull towards your glute. - Cue: "Tuck your tailbone under to feel a deeper stretch in the front of your hip."

warmups.name: Arm Circles
warmups.desc: Targets: Entire Deltoid Complex. Biomechanics: Lubricates the shoulder joint through its full range of motion. - Stand with arms out to your sides. - Make small circles, gradually getting bigger, then reverse. - Cue: "Draw perfect circles in the air with your fingertips."

warmups.name: Bodyweight Arm Crossovers
warmups.desc: Targets: Pectoralis Major & Deltoids. Biomechanics: Warms up the chest and shoulder tie-in. - Swing arms open wide, then cross them in front of your chest. - Alternate which arm is on top. - Cue: "Focus on a brief stretch as you open your arms wide."

compound.name: 30-Degree Incline DB Chest Press
compound.desc: Targets: Pectoralis Major (Clavicular Head). Biomechanics: Incline aligns press with upper chest fibers. - Lie on a 30-degree bench. Press dumbbells up from chest level. - Keep your shoulder blades pinned back and down on the bench. - Cue: "Drive your biceps towards each other at the top of the press."

isolations.name: Low-to-High Cable Crossover
isolations.desc: Targets: Pectoralis Major (Clavicular Head). Biomechanics: Upward arc mimics the function of upper pectoral fibers. - Set cables to the lowest position. Grab handles and step forward. - With a slight bend in your elbows, bring hands upward and together to shoulder height. - Cue: "Scoop the air up and in."

isolations.name: Overhead Cable Triceps Extension
isolations.desc: Targets: Triceps Brachii (Long Head). Biomechanics: Stretches the long head at the start for maximal load. - Face away from the cable machine. Grab rope from behind your head. - Extend arms straight up, keeping elbows stationary. - Cue: "Pin your elbows to your head; only your forearms should move."

cooldowns.name: Doorway Chest Stretch
cooldowns.desc: Targets: Pectoralis Major & Minor. Biomechanics: Lengthens pectoral fibers away from the origin. - Place forearms on a doorframe, elbows slightly below shoulders. - Step forward until you feel a stretch in your chest. - Cue: "Lead with your chest, not your chin."

cooldowns.name: Overhead Triceps Stretch
cooldowns.desc: Targets: Triceps Brachii (Long Head). Biomechanics: Maximally lengthens the long head of the triceps. - Raise one arm, bend elbow to drop hand behind head. - Use other hand to gently pull the elbow. - Cue: "Reach your hand down your spine."

warmups.name: Bodyweight Forward Lunges
warmups.desc: Targets: Quadriceps & Gluteus Maximus. Biomechanics: Primes the primary movers for the lunge pattern. - Step forward, lowering hips until both knees are at 90-degree angles. - Push off the front foot to return to start. - Cue: "Keep your front knee aligned with your second toe."

warmups.name: High-Knee March
warmups.desc: Targets: Hip Flexors. Biomechanics: Neural stimulation without metabolic fatigue. - March in place, driving one knee up at a time. - Stay on the balls of your feet. - Cue: "Pull your knee to your chest using your abs."

compound.name: Walking Lunge
compound.desc: Targets: Gluteus Maximus & Quadriceps. Biomechanics: Hip and knee extension to propel the body forward. - Step forward and lower hips until rear knee nearly touches the ground. - Push off the rear foot to step into the next lunge. - Cue: "Think of it as falling forward with control."

isolations.name: Lying Leg Curl Machine
isolations.desc: Targets: Hamstring Group. Biomechanics: Isolates knee flexion function of the hamstrings. - Lie face down, pad on lower calves. - Curl legs up towards your glutes. - Cue: "Squeeze your hamstrings at the top; imagine pulling your heel to your butt."

isolations.name: Standing Calf Raise
isolations.desc: Targets: Gastrocnemius. Biomechanics: Straight leg targets the upper portion of the calf. - Stand on the edge of a step. Let heels drop, then press up onto your toes. - Pause at the very top. - Cue: "Try to get your heel higher than your big toe."

cooldowns.name: Standing Wall Calf Stretch
cooldowns.desc: Targets: Gastrocnemius & Soleus. Biomechanics: Straight leg stretches the upper calf; bent knee stretches the lower. - Face a wall, step one foot back, keeping leg straight and heel on the floor. - Lean forward. - Cue: "Drive your back heel into the ground."

cooldowns.name: Seated Toe Touch
cooldowns.desc: Targets: Hamstring Group. Biomechanics: Lengthens hamstrings from insertion to origin. - Sit with legs straight. Hinge at your hips and reach for your toes. - Keep your back straight. - Cue: "Lead with your chest, not by rounding your back."

warmups.name: Cat-Cow
warmups.desc: Targets: Erector Spinae & Rectus Abdominis. Biomechanics: Mobilizes and lubricates the spine. - On all fours, inhale as you drop your belly and look up (Cow). - Exhale as you round your spine (Cat). - Cue: "Initiate the movement from your tailbone."

warmups.name: Arm Swings
warmups.desc: Targets: Deltoid & Rotator Cuff. Biomechanics: Warms up the shoulder joint without load. - Stand with feet shoulder-width apart. - Swing arms forward and back, then side to side. - Cue: "Let the arms be heavy and relaxed, like ropes."

compound.name: Bent-Over Dumbbell Row
compound.desc: Targets: Latissimus Dorsi & Rhomboids. Biomechanics: Shoulder extension and scapular retraction. - Hinge at hips, back straight. Pull dumbbells up towards your ribcage. - Squeeze shoulder blades together at the top. - Cue: "Imagine pulling your elbows into your back pockets."

isolations.name: Single-Arm Concentration Curl
isolations.desc: Targets: Biceps Brachii. Biomechanics: Supination isolates the biceps peak. - Sit on a bench, brace your arm against your inner thigh. - Curl the weight up, turning your palm to face your shoulder at the top. - Cue: "Keep your upper arm vertical and still."

isolations.name: Cable Rear Delt Fly
isolations.desc: Targets: Posterior (Rear) Deltoid. Biomechanics: Horizontal abduction matches rear delt fiber orientation. - Set cables to shoulder height. Cross arms and pull outward and backward. - Keep a slight bend in your elbows. - Cue: "Imagine you are trying to stretch a giant rubber band across your chest."

cooldowns.name: Cross-Body Shoulder Stretch
cooldowns.desc: Targets: Posterior Deltoid & Rhomboids. Biomechanics: Stretches the rear shoulder muscles. - Pull one arm across your chest with the other. - Keep the shoulder down, away from your ear. - Cue: "Try to look over the shoulder you are stretching."

cooldowns.name: Child’s Pose
cooldowns.desc: Targets: Latissimus Dorsi & Erector Spinae. Biomechanics: Lengthens the entire back from hips to hands. - Kneel, sit back on your heels, and fold forward. - Rest your forehead on the floor with arms extended. - Cue: "Breathe into your back, feeling it expand with each inhale."

warmups.name: Glute Bridges
warmups.desc: Targets: Gluteus Maximus. Biomechanics: Pure hip extension for neural stimulation. - Lie on your back, knees bent. Lift your hips until your body is in a straight line. - Squeeze your glutes at the top. - Cue: "Drive through your heels."

warmups.name: Fire Hydrants
warmups.desc: Targets: Gluteus Medius & Minimus. Biomechanics: Hip abduction to activate side glutes. - On all fours, keep knee bent and lift one leg out to the side. - Keep your back flat. - Cue: "Imagine a glass of water on your lower back and don't spill it."

compound.name: Curtsy Lunge
compound.desc: Targets: Gluteus Medius & Adductors. Biomechanics: Diagonal movement forces glute medius to stabilize the hip. - Step one foot diagonally behind the other, bending both knees as if curtsying. - Keep your chest up. - Cue: "Focus on pushing off the front foot to return to the start."

isolations.name: Cable Glute Kickback
isolations.desc: Targets: Gluteus Maximus. Biomechanics: Isolates hip extension. - Attach ankle strap to a low cable. Kick the leg straight back. - Squeeze the glute of the working leg at the end of the movement. - Cue: "Don't just swing your leg; consciously squeeze the muscle to move the weight."

isolations.name: Seated Hip Abduction Machine
isolations.desc: Targets: Gluteus Medius & Minimus. Biomechanics: Direct resistance against hip abduction. - Sit tall, chest up. Drive the pads outward. - Control the return. - Cue: "Squeeze from the outside of your hips."

cooldowns.name: Figure-4 Stretch
cooldowns.desc: Targets: Glutes & Piriformis. Biomechanics: Stretches the deep external rotators of the hip. - Lie on your back, cross one ankle over the opposite knee. - Pull the bottom leg toward your chest. - Cue: "Keep your lower back pressed into the floor."

cooldowns.name: Pigeon Pose
cooldowns.desc: Targets: Glutes & Hip Flexors. Biomechanics: Deeply stretches the hip rotators and flexors. - From a plank, bring one knee forward to the outside of the same-side hand. - Extend the back leg. Fold forward if possible. - Cue: "Try to square your hips to the front."

warmups.name: Shoulder Shrugs
warmups.desc: Targets: Upper Trapezius. Biomechanics: Scapular elevation to prep the shoulder girdle. - Stand tall, lift your shoulders up toward your ears, then lower. - Cue: "Imagine trying to touch your ears with your shoulders."

warmups.name: Wall Angels
warmups.desc: Targets: Rotator Cuff & Scapular Stabilizers. Biomechanics: Trains scapular control and external rotation. - Stand with your back against a wall. Slide your arms up and down the wall. - Keep your elbows and wrists in contact with the wall. - Cue: "Don't let your lower back arch away from the wall."

compound.name: Standing Arnold Press
compound.desc: Targets: All three heads of the Deltoid. Biomechanics: Rotation + press engages all parts of the shoulder. - Start with palms facing you. Press overhead while rotating wrists so palms face forward. - Keep your core tight to avoid arching your back. - Cue: "Uncork your shoulders as you press up."

isolations.name: Dumbbell Lateral Raise
isolations.desc: Targets: Lateral Deltoid. Biomechanics: Pure shoulder abduction isolates the side delts. - Stand with a slight bend in your elbows. Raise weights straight out to your sides. - Stop at shoulder height. - Cue: "Lead with your elbows, not your hands. Imagine pushing two walls apart."

isolations.name: Cable Triceps Pushdown
isolations.desc: Targets: Triceps Brachii (Lateral & Medial Heads). Biomechanics: Pure elbow extension. - Keep elbows pinned to your sides. Push the bar down until arms are fully extended. - Cue: "Imagine your elbows are nailed to your ribcage."

cooldowns.name: Neck Lateral Flexion Stretch
cooldowns.desc: Targets: Upper Trapezius. Biomechanics: Lengthens the upper trap fibers. - Gently tilt your head to one side, bringing your ear toward your shoulder. - Do not force the stretch. - Cue: "Let the weight of your head do the stretching."

cooldowns.name: Behind-the-Back Shoulder Stretch
cooldowns.desc: Targets: Anterior Deltoid & Biceps. Biomechanics: Opens the chest and stretches the front of the shoulder. - Clasp hands behind your back and gently lift your arms away from your body. - Cue: "Squeeze your shoulder blades together as you lift."

warmups.name: Bodyweight Good Mornings
warmups.desc: Targets: Hamstrings & Erector Spinae. Biomechanics: Grooves the hip hinge pattern. - Stand with hands behind your head. Hinge at the hips, keeping your back straight. - Cue: "Push your butt back to the wall behind you."

warmups.name: Leg Swings (Front-to-Back)
warmups.desc: Targets: Hamstrings & Hip Flexors. Biomechanics: Dynamic stretch without upper body fatigue. - Hold onto a support. Swing one leg forward and back like a pendulum. - Cue: "Feel the stretch at the end of each swing."

compound.name: Romanian Deadlift
compound.desc: Targets: Hamstrings & Gluteus Maximus. Biomechanics: A pure hip-hinge that loads the hamstrings. - With a slight bend in your knees, push your hips backward to lower the weight. - Keep the bar close to your legs. - Cue: "Scrape your shins with the bar. Squeeze your glutes to stand back up."

isolations.name: Seated Leg Curl Machine
isolations.desc: Targets: Hamstring Group. Biomechanics: Isolates the knee flexion function of the hamstrings. - Keep your back against the pad. Pull heels backward and down. - Cue: "Try to dig your heels under the seat."

isolations.name: 45-Degree Back Extension
isolations.desc: Targets: Erector Spinae & Glutes. Biomechanics: Hip extension against gravity. - Lock your feet in. Lower your upper body, then use your glutes and lower back to pull your torso up until it's in line with your legs. - Cue: "This is a glute exercise. Squeeze your glutes to lift your body."

cooldowns.name: Downward Facing Dog
cooldowns.desc: Targets: Hamstrings, Calves, & Lats. Biomechanics: Stretches the entire posterior chain. - From all fours, lift your hips up and back. - "Pedal" your feet by bending one knee at a time. - Cue: "Push the ground away with your hands and try to get your chest closer to your thighs."

cooldowns.name: Supine Knee-to-Chest
cooldowns.desc: Targets: Glutes & Lower Back. Biomechanics: Gently relieves tension in the lower back. - Lie on your back and pull one knee into your chest. - Cue: "Try to keep your tailbone on the floor."

warmups.name: Light Weight Scapular Pulldowns
warmups.desc: Targets: Lower Trapezius & Lats. Biomechanics: Neural stimulation without grip fatigue. - Using a light weight, pull your shoulder blades down and back without bending your arms. - Cue: "Imagine you are trying to put your shoulder blades in your back pockets."

warmups.name: Torso Twists
warmups.desc: Targets: Obliques. Biomechanics: Warms up core stabilizers. - Stand with feet wide, holding a light weight or just your hands. - Rotate your torso from side to side. - Cue: "Follow your hands with your eyes."

compound.name: Lat Pulldown
compound.desc: Targets: Latissimus Dorsi. Biomechanics: Shoulder adduction and extension to build back width. - Take a wide grip. Pull the bar down to your upper chest. - Keep your chest up. - Cue: "Drive your elbows down towards the floor."

isolations.name: Straight Arm Cable Pulldown
isolations.desc: Targets: Latissimus Dorsi. Biomechanics: Isolates the shoulder extension function of the lats. - Using a straight bar, push the bar down in an arc to your thighs. - Keep your arms straight. - Cue: "Think of yourself as a human protractor, hinging only at the shoulder."

isolations.name: Dumbbell Hammer Curl
isolations.desc: Targets: Brachioradialis & Brachialis. Biomechanics: Neutral grip builds forearm and upper arm thickness. - Hold dumbbells with a neutral (palms facing in) grip. - Curl the weights toward your shoulders. - Cue: "Imagine you are holding a hammer."

cooldowns.name: Overhead Lat Stretch
cooldowns.desc: Targets: Latissimus Dorsi. Biomechanics: Lengthens the lats from insertion to origin. - Reach one arm overhead, grasp the wrist with your other hand, and gently pull to the side. - Cue: "Reach up before you reach over."

cooldowns.name: Wrist Flexor/Extensor Stretch
cooldowns.desc: Targets: Forearm Flexors & Extensors. Biomechanics: Lengthens the small muscles of the forearm. - Extend one arm, palm up. Use the other hand to gently pull the fingers back. - Flip your hand over and repeat. - Cue: "Keep your elbow straight."
